title: Verilog Preprocessor Extension for Variadic Arguments
body:
Current Verilog preprocessor directives (define, ifdef, include, etc.) lack support for variadic arguments, limiting code modularity and increasing boilerplate in parameterized macros. Many modern hardware projects strive for reusable, parameter-driven design patterns — similar to what variadic templates enable in C++ or variadic macros in C.

Adding support for variadic macros (e.g., \define MY_MACRO(...)`) would dramatically improve code flexibility, readability, and maintainability in Verilog/SystemVerilog projects.

Some usage case:

Parameterized Logging and Debugging
Today:
`define LOG1(x) $display(x)
`define LOG2(x,y) $display(x,y)

With variadic macros:
`define LOG(...) $display(__VA_ARGS__)

...
`LOG("A=%0d, B=%0d", a, b)
`LOG("Hello")
Cleaner, more C-like usage — and just one macro instead of many overloads.

2. Flexible Assertions
`define ASSERT(cond, ...) if (!(cond)) $fatal(1, "Assertion failed: " __VA_ARGS__)
Usage: `ASSERT(a == b, "Mismatch: a=%0d, b=%0d", a, b)

3. General-Purpose Macros with Optional Parameters
`define TRACE(...) `ifdef ENABLE_TRACE $display(__VA_ARGS__); `endif

4. Macro-Based FSMs or Instruction Decoders
Creating compact, readable FSMs or instruction decoders:
`define INSTR(OP, ...) \
  OP: begin __VA_ARGS__ end

case (opcode)
  `INSTR(ADD, result = a + b;)
  `INSTR(SUB, result = a - b;)
endcase
